steps.title: Identify the shared prerequisite
steps.content: Text 1 calls root-to-root separation an “essential step” and Text 2 calls it “the necessary precaution.” Despite disagreeing on whether Wang's full design succeeds, both authors agree that separating root systems is a required foundation for properly examining whether a CMN transfers nutrients.

steps.title: Match the prerequisite to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C directly states what both authors treat as a prerequisite: without precluding root-to-root transfer, determining the CMN mechanism is impossible.
The other three either invent unsupported claims (A), confuse necessity with sufficiency (B), or impose unstated requirements (D).
That matches choice C — C is correct.
